Title: Two Sum
Description: Find two numbers whose sum equals the target.
Answer: Use a HashMap to store complements while traversing the array.
InterviewTip: Explain brute force first then optimize using HashMap.

Title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
Description: Find the maximum profit from one stock transaction.
Answer: Track the minimum price seen so far and calculate maximum profit.
InterviewTip: Mention why only one transaction is allowed.

Title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
Description: Find the longest substring containing unique characters.
Answer: Use the sliding window technique with a HashMap to maintain unique characters.
InterviewTip: Explain how the left pointer moves when duplicates are found.

Title: Reverse Linked List
Description: Reverse a singly linked list.
Answer: Iteratively reverse node pointers using previous current and next references.
InterviewTip: Mention both iterative and recursive approaches.

Title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree
Description: Find the lowest common ancestor of two nodes.
Answer: Use recursive DFS to locate nodes and return the common ancestor.

Title: Clone Graph
Description: Create a deep copy of an undirected graph.
Answer: Use DFS or BFS with a HashMap to avoid cloning nodes multiple times.

Title: Top K Frequent Elements
Description: Return the k most frequent elements.
Answer: Use a HashMap for frequencies and a Min Heap of size k.
InterviewTip: Mention Bucket Sort as an alternative.

Title: Daily Temperatures
Description: Find the number of days until a warmer temperature.
Answer: Use a monotonic decreasing stack to store indices.
InterviewTip: Explain why indices are stored instead of values.

Title: Rotting Oranges
Description: Determine the minimum time required to rot all oranges.
Answer: Use Multi-Source BFS by pushing all rotten oranges into a queue initially.

Title: House Robber
Description: Find the maximum money that can be robbed without robbing adjacent houses.
Answer: Use dynamic programming by storing the maximum profit at each step.
InterviewTip: Explain the state transition before coding.

Title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
Description: Search a target in a rotated sorted array.
Answer: Use modified binary search by determining which half is sorted in each iteration.
InterviewTip: Clearly explain how to identify the sorted half.

Title: Jump Game
Description: Determine whether you can reach the last index.
Answer: Track the farthest reachable index while traversing the array.
InterviewTip: Explain why greedy works instead of dynamic programming.

Title: LRU Cache
Description: Design an LRU Cache supporting O(1) operations.
Answer: Use a HashMap with a Doubly Linked List to maintain recently used items.
InterviewTip: Mention why LinkedHashMap works in Java.

Title: Second Highest Salary
Description: Write a query to find the second highest salary.
Answer: Use ORDER BY with LIMIT OFFSET or a subquery using MAX().

Title: Database Normalization
Description: Explain normalization and its types.
Answer: Discuss 1NF 2NF 3NF BCNF and how they reduce redundancy.

Title: SOLID Principles
Description: Explain SOLID principles with examples.
Answer: Describe each principle with a real-world software example.
InterviewTip: Focus on maintainable and scalable code.

Title: Design Amazon Locker
Description: Design a scalable Amazon Locker system.
Answer: Discuss APIs database locker allocation QR verification notifications and scalability.
InterviewTip: Think about availability and concurrent users.

Title: Design an E-Commerce Website
Description: Design an e-commerce platform similar to Amazon.
Answer: Explain product catalog inventory search payments orders caching and load balancing.
InterviewTip: Always discuss scalability and fault tolerance.

Title: TCP vs UDP
Description: Explain the differences between TCP and UDP.
Answer: Compare reliability speed packet ordering congestion control and use cases.
InterviewTip: Give real-world examples like HTTP and video streaming.

Title: REST API Design
Description: How would you design a REST API for an online shopping application?
Answer: Explain REST principles HTTP methods authentication pagination status codes and versioning.
InterviewTip: Always mention proper status codes and security.

Title: Tell me about yourself
Description: Introduce yourself professionally.
Answer: Start with your education then discuss your internship projects technical skills achievements and explain why you want to join Amazon.
InterviewTip: Keep your answer under two minutes and focus on relevant experience.

Title: Why Amazon?
Description: Explain why you want to join Amazon.
Answer: Discuss Amazon's customer obsession innovation culture global impact and opportunities for learning and growth.
InterviewTip: Never mention salary as the primary reason.

Title: Why should we hire you?
Description: Explain why you are the right candidate.
Answer: Highlight your technical skills problem solving ability internship experience adaptability and willingness to learn.

Title: What are your strengths?
Description: Describe your strengths.
Answer: Discuss analytical thinking teamwork adaptability communication and problem solving with examples.

Title: What is your biggest weakness?
Description: Discuss a genuine weakness.
Answer: Mention a real weakness explain how you are improving it and what you learned.
InterviewTip: Never say you have no weaknesses.

Title: Describe a situation where you showed ownership
Description: Explain how you took ownership of a task.
Answer: Describe a project where you accepted responsibility beyond your assigned work and delivered successful results.
InterviewTip: Ownership is one of Amazon's Leadership Principles.

Title: How do you handle constructive criticism?
Description: Explain your response to feedback.
Answer: Describe how you accept feedback improve yourself and continuously learn.

Title: Where do you see yourself in five years?
Description: Describe your future goals.
Answer: Discuss becoming a skilled software engineer taking ownership of impactful products and mentoring others.
InterviewTip: Align your goals with Amazon's growth opportunities.

Title: Describe yourself in three words
Description: Choose three words describing yourself.
Answer: Select qualities like Curious Responsible and Adaptable with supporting examples.

Title: How do you prioritize your work?
Description: Explain how you manage multiple tasks.
Answer: Discuss prioritization using deadlines business impact and planning tools while communicating with stakeholders.

Title: Tell me about a time you demonstrated Customer Obsession
Description: Describe a situation where customer satisfaction was your priority.
Answer: Use the STAR method to explain how your actions improved customer experience.
InterviewTip: Customer Obsession is Amazon's most important Leadership Principle.

Title: Describe a time you took complete ownership of a project
Description: Explain how you accepted responsibility.
Answer: Describe the challenge your actions and measurable results using the STAR framework.

Title: Tell me about a time you failed
Description: Describe a failure and what you learned.
Answer: Accept responsibility explain improvements and focus on lessons learned.

Title: Describe a difficult decision you had to make
Description: Explain how you handled a challenging decision.
Answer: Describe how you analyzed options evaluated risks and reached the best outcome.

Title: Describe a conflict with a teammate
Description: Explain how you resolved disagreement.
Answer: Discuss active listening communication and collaborative problem solving.

Title: Tell me about a time you handled multiple deadlines
Description: Describe how you managed competing priorities.
Answer: Explain task prioritization communication planning and successful execution.

Title: Describe a time you had to convince someone to accept your idea
Description: Explain how you influenced others.
Answer: Use data logical reasoning and clear communication to gain support.

Title: Describe a time you improved a process
Description: Explain how you identified and solved an inefficiency.
Answer: Describe the improvement implementation and measurable impact.

Title: Tell me about a time you learned a new technology quickly
Description: Explain how you adapted to change.
Answer: Describe your learning strategy implementation and project outcome.
InterviewTip: Amazon values Learn and Be Curious.

Title: Describe a situation where you delivered results under pressure
Description: Explain how you handled pressure.
Answer: Describe planning execution teamwork and successful delivery using the STAR method.

Title: Profit and Loss
Description: Calculate profit percentage in a business scenario.
Answer: Apply the Profit Percentage formula and verify calculations carefully.

Title: Time Speed and Distance
Description: Solve a train crossing problem.
Answer: Use distance speed and time relationships to calculate the answer.

Title: Probability
Description: Calculate the probability of an event.
Answer: Use favorable outcomes divided by total outcomes.

Title: Time and Work
Description: Determine the time required to complete work together.
Answer: Use work efficiency concepts and LCM methods.

Title: Blood Relations
Description: Determine family relationships from given information.
Answer: Draw a family tree to simplify the relationships.

Title: Seating Arrangement
Description: Solve a circular seating arrangement problem.
Answer: Represent positions visually and eliminate invalid possibilities.

Title: Number Series
Description: Identify the missing number in a sequence.
Answer: Recognize arithmetic geometric or mixed patterns.

Title: Synonyms and Antonyms
Description: Choose the correct synonym or antonym.
Answer: Use vocabulary knowledge and contextual meaning.

Title: Professional Email Writing
Description: Write a professional email responding to a customer.
Answer: Use a clear subject greeting concise content and professional closing.

Title: Presentation Skills
Description: Present a technical project to business stakeholders.
Answer: Focus on business impact achievements architecture and future improvements using simple language.
